poland.title: Legalizing in Poland funds from Ukrainian property sales
poland.description: Property sale in UA → legal funds in a Polish bank. Two methods, tax and document calculator.
poland.metaTitle: Legalizing in Poland funds from Ukrainian property sales
poland.subtitle: Legalization in PL: amount after UA taxes, two transfer methods, bank document package

netSection.title: After taxes in Ukraine
netSection.hint: Enter the amount after taxes in Ukraine — legalization methods in Poland below are calculated from it. Not linked to the calculator in the next block.

taxCalcAccordion.title: Tax calculator for selling in Ukraine
taxCalcAccordion.subtitle: Separate — does not change the amount above
taxCalcAccordion.independentNote: Fields here are independent of “After taxes in Ukraine”. They only calculate sale costs in UA; you can manually copy the result into the field above.

border.title: Method 1: via the border
border.intro: Withdraw hryvnia in Ukraine, exchange to USD, carry across the border with UA and PL customs declarations, deposit at the bank on arrival day.

atm.title: Method 2: ATM + branch counter
atm.short: UA card → ATM in PL → deposit
atm.intro: Funds stay on a Ukrainian card (hryvnia account). Withdraw at a Polish ATM, keep the receipt, then deposit cash at your bank’s counter with the same property-sale document package — without carrying currency across the border.
steps: Prepare the source-of-funds package: sale contract (UA), tax payment proof, statements from UA accounts.
Withdraw cash at a Polish bank ATM with a Ukrainian card — keep the receipt (date, amount, ATM).
On the withdrawal day or soon after, deposit cash at your Polish bank’s counter, presenting documents and ATM receipts.

UA card limits require several weekly cycles — this is not “structuring”. Do not withdraw below the limit “to stay under the radar” or add extra operations; each cycle needs an ATM receipt, counter deposit, and the same document package.
atm.weeklyLimitHint: Limit in hryvnia on the card account; at a Polish ATM you receive PLN at the network rate.

verdict.label: Summary for method 2
verdict.body: After taxes you need ${{net}}. With {{cards}} {{cardsWord}} and {{weeklyUah}} UAH/week per card (≈ ${{perWeekUsd}}/week, total {{perWeekUah}} UAH) — {{weeks}} {{weeksWord}} of ATM + counter cycles. No customs declarations required.
verdict.highlight: Key evidence: ATM receipts + bank counter deposit slip + UA sale contract and tax proof.

atm.notSmurfingIntro: Smurfing means splitting deposits on purpose to evade reporting thresholds without a transparent source. Method 2 can look similar externally because of the UA card limit — but the logic is different if you document it.
notSmurfingPoints: Source is known upfront: UA sale contract, tax paid, statements — funds are already yours on a Ukrainian card after the sale, not “cash from nowhere”.
Number of cycles is dictated by the withdrawal limit (UAH/week per card), not by keeping each deposit “below the bank threshold”.
Each week — ATM → counter pair: withdrawal receipt + deposit receipt; amounts match, same document package.
One owner, one chain: no third-party accounts, no “round-trip” transfers, no extra small operations beyond the limit.
Notify the bank early (counter / compliance): property sale in UA, approximate weeks and amount per limit — preferably in writing or on the KYC form.
atm.notSmurfingNote: Bank and GIIF may ask questions about a series of deposits — answer: card limit + documents for each cycle (not smurfing from the “Schemes” section below).
notes: Real cost: ATM fees (PL + UA card issuer), UAH→PLN rate worse than NBU — over many weeks losses can exceed savings from skipping border trips (compare with method 1 in the calculator).
Main limit — UA card withdrawal cap (week/day, sometimes a separate “abroad” limit); the bank may temporarily block a series of large ATM withdrawals without prior notice.
You need an already opened account at a Polish bank; the counter may refuse deposit without a full package or if the account balance does not match the explained source.
Each cycle — withdrawal and deposit in a short window; avoid “stockpiling” cash between weeks — theft risk and questions why deposit was not on the ATM day.
Process takes months — hryvnia devaluation on the card and changing bank rules; plan cycles with the bank at the start (see “Why this is not smurfing”).

verdict.label: Summary for method 1
verdict.body: After taxes: ${{net}}. Hryvnia withdrawal at {{banks}} {{banksWord}} — {{days}} {{daysWord}}. Transport: {{trips}} {{tripsWord}} (up to ${{perTrip}} cash per trip), PL bank deposit — roughly {{weeksMin}}–{{weeksMax}} weeks including preparation.
verdict.highlight: Per trip: 2nd copy of exchange receipt + UA and PL customs declarations + bank deposit on crossing day.

trips.desc: UA declaration + PL declaration · bank deposit on arrival day

border.label: Bring on first deposit
items: 2nd copy of UAH→USD exchange receipts (each trip)
Copies of Ukrainian customs declarations (each trip)
Copies of Polish customs declarations (each trip)

atm.label: Bring when depositing at the counter
items: Statements from Ukrainian account / card (source of funds)
ATM receipts for each withdrawal in Poland
Bank counter cash deposit receipts (each deposit)
Written explanation to the bank: sale in UA, planned cycles per card limit (date, approximate amounts)

reasons.label: LAW · STRUCTURING
reasons.text: A series of small deposits below reporting thresholds without economic rationale is treated as podejrzenie prania pieniędzy (AML Act 2018, incl. art. 74 — reporting duty). This is not legal “splitting” of apartment sale proceeds — even a lawful source does not remove AML checks.

links.text: Criminal Code art. 299 — money laundering (up to 8 years)

reasons.label: THRESHOLDS · BANKS
reasons.text: Banks must analyze cash operations and deposit series and pass information to GIIF (incl. transactions above EUR 15,000, suspicious patterns). Small deposits “under the threshold” are often aggregated in one client profile — not anonymity.
reasons.quote: „Rozbicie kwoty nie tylko nie uchroni przed raportem do GIIF, ale niemal gwarantuje oznaczenie operacji czerwoną flagą.”

reasons.label: GIIF 2024 · FIGURES
reasons.text: GIIF 2024 report: 8,129 SAR (+71% vs typical ~4.5k/year); 4,765 analytical cases (+38.4%); 641 blocked accounts (+39%, ~PLN 148.5M); 532 reports to authorities (KAS, police, etc.); 50.34M threshold transactions + 270,200 corrective notices.

reasons.label: SANCTIONS
reasons.text: Administrative: fine up to 75% of the transaction amount (Fiscal Penal Code art. 54) for AML duty breaches. Criminal: Criminal Code art. 299 (6 months to 8 years) + other articles depending on role. Account blocking and freezing — without court at the AML stage.

links.text: Fiscal Penal Code art. 54 — 75% monetary penalty

reasons.label: DELAYED RISK
reasons.text: NIK (2024): GIIF opens analytical cases on average ~360 days after the event; data kept 5 years. So “half a year passed” does not mean “forgotten”.

reasons.label: PCC · WHEN TAX ARISES
reasons.text: Pożyczka as a civil-law act — PCC 0.5% (Title V of the PCC Act), paid by the borrower. Tax arises when the contract is concluded in PL or when the loan object (money) is in Poland. “Contract abroad” without PCC only if both: contract outside PL and funds outside PL at signing — otherwise 0.5% on the full amount.

reasons.label: SHAM LOAN · CRIMINAL CODE
reasons.text: A loan without real repayment, forged contract, or a fake lender — Criminal Code art. 271 (document forgery, up to 5 years) and possible art. 299 (money laundering). The bank still requires source explanation on cash deposit: who lent, where their money came from, why cash, why not a direct transfer from the property sale.

links.text: pit.pl — PCC and location of funds

reasons.label: AML · BANK
reasons.text: Obligated institutions must identify the beneficial owner and source of funds (AML Act). A loan from an acquaintance without banking trail (withdrawals, transfers, declarations) often ends in refusal to accept the deposit or a SAR to GIIF — same as with “simple” cash.

links.text: Ministry of Finance — AML institution duties

reasons.label: THRESHOLD · EU
reasons.text: When crossing the EU external border, cash from the equivalent of EUR 10,000 (all currencies together, traveller’s cheques, gold, etc.) must be declared in writing to KAS. In Poland also electronic declaration via PUESC before crossing. Not “10k per person” — total per person in one crossing.

reasons.label: SANCTIONS · PL
reasons.text: Fiscal Penal Code art. 106d: false declaration or failure to declare — fine up to 720 daily rates; for larger amounts — criminal liability, confiscation of funds. Art. 106f/106h — additional offences on import/export. KAS practice: confiscation + fine up to 50% of undeclared cash.

links.text: Fiscal Penal Code art. 106f — failure to declare
Fiscal Penal Code art. 106d — false declaration

reasons.label: SANCTIONS · UA
reasons.text: Export from Ukraine without declaration (above set limits) — confiscation, fine up to 20% (UA tax code), criminal liability for currency smuggling. Double border UA→PL = two controls.

reasons.label: CASE · INFOR 2024
reasons.text: KAS: Ukrainian citizen entered with ~PLN 17M cash, declared only EUR 20k — case of illegal import and evasion of declaration.

reasons.label: CASE · PODKARPACIE 2024
reasons.text: Subcarpathian KAS unit (2024): 130+ cases of illegal cash movement, over EUR 2.6M seized. Typical scenario — no declaration or underreporting on crossing from UA.

reasons.label: TRANSPARENCY · GIIF
reasons.text: On-chain analysis (Chainalysis, Reactor, etc.) — GIIF and banks receive reports on risky wallets. “Bought USDT — sold for PLN” does not remove source questions: on fiat withdrawal the bank requires the same package as for cash (contract, taxes, statements).

reasons.label: KYC · BITOMATY · VASP
reasons.text: From 2024–2025 Polish VASPs and crypto ATMs under tighter supervision: client identification, limits, reporting to GIIF (MiCA / AML Act). “No questions” exchange for large amounts is practically over.

reasons.label: CRIMINAL · CRIMINAL CODE
reasons.text: Money laundering (Criminal Code art. 299) — 6 months to 8 years; higher rates for organized groups. Cryptocurrencies in Polish cases 2024–2025 are often linked to human trafficking and mule transfers.

reasons.label: CASE · CBŚP 2025
reasons.text: Central Bureau of Investigation (2025): 10 Ukrainian citizens detained in Warsaw, Łódź, Wrocław; ~30 suspects in money laundering via cryptocurrencies (linked to human trafficking); 12 convicted at earlier stages.

reasons.label: MECHANICS · PATTERN
reasons.text: Money mule: cash deposit / incoming transfer to your account → quick transfer onward (often abroad or to crypto). Bank sees “deposit without income” + “immediate outflow”. Even without bad intent — risk of Criminal Code art. 299 (facilitating laundering).

reasons.label: LIABILITY
reasons.text: Criminal Code art. 299 §1: 6 months – 8 years (§4 up to 10 years in special circumstances). “They paid me PLN 500” is not a defence — court assesses whether you could not have known the criminal source. Using another person’s account to “legalize” apartment sale proceeds makes you a link without contract protection.

reasons.label: OPERATION · POLAND 2023
reasons.text: Police / Central Bureau of Investigation (2023): large anti–money mule operation found 10,759 people in schemes; 1,013 arrests; significant share — foreigners, including Ukrainians in Poland (recruitment via Telegram/OLX).

reasons.label: BANK · GIIF
reasons.text: Bank algorithms flag: cash deposit → fast SWIFT/SEPA transfer → account closed. GIIF receives SAR; account blocked pending explanation. For legalizing property sale proceeds a mule is not needed — you need your own account and documents.

reasons.label: NOTARIES · AML
reasons.text: Since 2022 notaries in Poland are AML obligated institutions (act + Ministry of Justice regulation). Sham deed, understated price, backdating — risk of refusal to certify and report to GIIF. Registry data is cross-checked with KAS and banks.

reasons.label: CRIMINAL CODE · ARTICLES
reasons.text: Document forgery (Criminal Code art. 271 — up to 5 years) + money laundering (art. 299 — up to 8 years) may cumulate; in serious cases (organized group) — higher limits. KAS: comparison of declared income with sham contracts.

reasons.label: CASE · PRZEMYŚL 2025
reasons.text: Border Guard, December 2025: 8 Ukrainian citizens convicted; ~PLN 2.5M + EUR 316,000 “laundered” via 27 accounts at 8 banks; scheme period 2020–2023; sham contracts and transfers.

reasons.label: PRACTICE · BANK
reasons.text: A Polish bank accepts a UA property sale contract only if: notarized form, tax paid, statements, logical chain (UA account → withdrawal/transfer → PL). “Contract for 50,000 but deposited 200,000” or unregistered contract — refusal or SAR.
